figures.caption: TABLE I REGRESSION-PLANNER RESULTS ON NUSCENES VAL . ♢ DENOTES EGO-STATUS INPUTS FOLLOWING BEVPLANNER++ [33]; ∗ DENOTES OFFICIALLY RELEASED MODELS. L2 IS PER-SECOND ENDPOINT DISPLACEMENT WITH AVG AS THE SIMPLE MEAN OF THE THREE HORIZONS; CR IS REPORTED AS THE PERCENTAGE OF TRAJECTORIES THAT COLLIDE WITHIN THE CORRESPONDING HORIZON.

Fig. 1. Overview of the PLAN-S framework. (a) The frozen perception encoder and latent world model produce current and future bird’s-eye-view (BEV) features. (b) The trainable cost-map decoder produces a four-channel semantic cost map from the BEV latent, conditioned on ego state and driving style via dual AdaFiLM. The cost map then guides planning through matched coupling interfaces for anchor-score and regression planners.
TABLE V ANCHOR-SCORE RESULTS ON NAVSIM NAVTEST . C: CAMERA; L: LIDAR; EGO: EGO-STATE ONLY. BOLD DENOTES THE BEST NON-HUMAN RESULT OR TIES.

Fig. 4. Style-conditioned cost-map comparison on a NAVSIM navtest scene. Top: front-view scene context. Bottom: BEV cost-map overlays under conservative, neutral, and aggressive style codes. Red dashed circles highlight regions where the spatial preference encoded by the cost map changes visibly across driving styles.

Fig. 3. Qualitative comparison on a NAVSIM navtest scene. Top: multiview inputs with the front-view trajectory overlay (green: model prediction, red: GT). Bottom: BEV outputs for DiffusionDrive, PLAN-S, and PLAN-S with the learned cost map overlaid; red tones denote lower-cost regions, and blue tones denote higher-cost regions.
